Remark : The result obtained by all the three methods is the same. So the choice of
method to be used depends on the numerical values of x, and f. If x, and f a
are
sufficiently
small, then the direct method is an appropriate choice. If x, and f, a
are
numerically 1
large numbers, then we can
go for the assumed r
mean method c
or
step-deviation method. If the class sizes are unequal, and x. are large numerically, we
can still a
 apply the step-deviation method by taking h to be a suitable divisor of all the d' s.
